title: The Big Cheese Ultra Power All-Metal Selfset Mouse Trap – Twinpack, Weatherproof, Galvanised Steel, Powerful Mechanism, Reusable, Self-Setting
features: GALVANISED METAL: A value pack of two long-lasting and hardwearing all-weather galvanised metal traps, The twinpack offers good coverage of both outdoor and indoor areas
STRONG: High performance snap and hold with integrated sure-grip spikes
POWERFUL: Performance tested to ensure effective control of mice – just like the traps that professionals use
SELF-SETTING: No-fuss mechanism makes setting simple and protects fingers, For best results, bait with The Big Cheese Mouse and Rat Attractant (STV163, sold separately)
TESTED AND PROVEN: On-pack guarantee provided
HYGIENIC: The metal surface is easy to wipe clean after disposal of captures allowing for re-use of the trap
description: From the manufacturer GUARANTEED EFFECTIVE RODENT CONTROL Bait & Kill: Our Ultra Power range offers the only true single-feed rodent poison available on the amateur market. The highly palatable formula contains a powerful active ingredient that delivers a lethal dose to rodents in just one feed. Catch & Kill: We offer a selection of versatile rodent trap solutions that are highly effective, with satisfaction guaranteed. Our powerful snap traps have been designed with durability in mind to be used again and again, whilst our innovative electronic products use smart-circuit technology to deliver a quick and humane kill. Pet-safe and child-safe options are available. Catch Alive: Safe for use around children and pets, these traps provide a humane, welfare-friendly alternative to lethal methods. Our professional-quality traps have been designed with safety in mind, both for the user and the target pest. Self-setting multi-catch mechanisms and fast-action closures allow for efficient rodent trapping and are ideal for large rodent infestations. Repel: 24/7 protection and peace of mind - our advanced mega-sonic repellers will protect your home around the clock, ridding your home of rodent pests and preventing their return. Poison-free and safe for use around children. Pack contains 2 traps GUARANTEED RODENT CONTROL Our on-pack pledge is "satisfaction guaranteed" reassuring you that the high-performance Ultra Power products from The Big Cheese will deliver effective results every time! The Big Cheese Ultra Power range delivers home-use rodent control solutions that are guaranteed effective - JUST LIKE THE PROFESSIONALS USE. Professional-quality products you can trust. Experts in rodent control for over 25 years. Ultra Power All-Metal Selfset Mouse Traps - 2pk from The Big Cheese All-weather galvanised trap. Original self-setting trap used for generations. Ultra Power All-Metal Selfset Traps from The Big Cheese are engineered to deliver effective control of rodent pests indoors and out – just like the professionals use. All-Metal Selfset Mouse Traps have been trusted by generations of users. They are easy to set, suitable for use in any weather conditions, and they are guaranteed to kill mice. Durable galvanised metal construction for indoor and outdoor use. High performance snap and hold. No-fuss mechanism makes setting simple and protects fingers. Strong, all-weather galvanised steel. Indoor and outdoor use. Bait with The Big Cheese Fresh 'n Tasty Rat & Mouse Attractant purchased separately. Part of the ULTRA POWER range. All-weather galvanised Engineered to deliver effective control of rodent pests indoors and out. Selfset traps are made from durable all-weather galvanised steel and have been trusted by generations of users suitable for use in any weather conditions. Powerful Ultra Power All-Metal Selfset Mouse Traps from The Big Cheese are a high performance trap – just like the traps the professionals use. Strong and powerful delivering an instant humane kill. CAUTION: THESE TRAPS ARE POWERFUL. Always locate them in protected areas. Keep them away from children, pets and non-target animals. It is an offence to locate the traps in areas which may endanger protected birds or animals. Direction for use For best results use several traps and set in pairs at 2–3 metre intervals along walls and runways, or where mice are active. Lift the fly over arm from the bait assembly and lay off the trap. Place bait firmly on the prongs. Locate the trap where mice activity has been seen, placing trap with bait facing towards the wall. Now simply pull back the striker wire to knock the fly over arm and return it to link with the baited assembly again. Release pressure on strike wire slowly. The trap is now set. Reusable Robust all-weather galvanised trap that is designed for repeat use. To increase catch rate, bait traps with The Big Cheese Fresh 'n Tasty Mouse and Rat Attractant (purchase separately). TRAPPING TIPS Block entry holes and target potential sources of rodent infestation such as garages and garden sheds. Remove other food sources from the treatment area. Store foodstuffs securely and practice good hygiene to avoid further infestations. Guaranteed effective rodent control Mice Mice are a highly adaptive and invasive species and they’re able to populate a variety of ecosystems. Agricultural fields, buildings, and boats are all viable habitats. In more temperate climates there will be even more possible nest sites. Swimming, climbing, jumping proficiency, and the two incisors on their upper jaw, all make for a formidable predator wherever they reside. However, despite all of these amazing abilities, the house mouse chooses to reside, as its name would indicate, in a home. House mice ( Mus Musculus Domesticus ) are a highly adaptive member of the genus Mus (mouse), yet for some reason they choose to live in the least demanding environment, a human home. They are attracted by the continuous flow of food and warmth, ideal for nest sites. The offer of food and shelter is so tempting that many choose to stay and breed year-round. Diet As one can imagine, the palate of a house mouse is highly adaptable since every habitat they migrate through will hold different opportunities for feeding. Outside dwellers will eat insects and a lot of grain, however, inside dwelling mice are practically omnivorous and, just like brown rats, will consume 10-15% of their body weight each day in just about anything we throw away. Most house mice will nibble on multiple different food sources to obtain their required nutrition, up to 20 times each night, since they’re nocturnal. A large proportion of a mouse’s 3g daily intake will be grain, namely cereal, favouring high-energy, man-made foods. Studies have proven that the levels of aggression in both rats and mice can be attributed to this high fat ‘human’ diet they have both been on for so long. These foods increase oestrogen levels and results in a shorter temper and longer periods of aggression. Evidence of intrusion 3-6 mm long droppings, black and rod-shaped. Approximately 80 per day, often appearing in clusters. Spotted in various places including attics, storage boxes, floorboards, disused cupboards. Check around fridges, ovens, heating units and washing machines too. Check wood, paper, cables, carpet and furniture for marks. Dark grey marks on surfaces from oil on hair. Although they don’t sweat, they emit a musky odour and urinate a lot, producing an ammonia smell. Hearing pattering feet in walls at night could be a sign of a mouse or rat infestation. If your dog is barking at night at what seems to be nothing, it could be picking up the scent or sounds of rodents.
